title: Generated Help, Structured Results, Output Channels, and Accessibility · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A delayed maintainer review reconstructs generated help, structured results, output channels, and accessibility from revision, runtime, command, unknown input, validation result, request identity, response fixture, promise state, cache decision, output, abort, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for accessible deterministic terminal view layer after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, caus…
objectives: Generate overview and command help from registry metadata with usage, examples, defaults, invalid forms, and close instructions.
Return a discriminated command result for data, usage, recoverable error, quit, and aborted outcomes before presentation.
Separate structured data, human status, diagnostics, and exit behavior through injected writers and stable formatting.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
review case — A review TypeScript team handles case 5875 during an offline mobile-library catalog migration. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to verify generated help, typed results, stable formatting, output routing, and accessible status; browser TypeScript must remain deterministic and free of Node or network effects; then change one unknown JSON field, nested member, status, URL, or byte budget and re-run validation. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Test ordering, empty and unknown fields, Unicode, control characters, no-color mode, narrow width, and stdout or stderr routing.
